protocol_notes: Run in English per Artem's instruction (instrument's Russian framing was for the run-sheet/debrief only, not a requirement for this specific run).

No in-session feedback given. Two items (12, 15) show substitution/avoidance — the error-correction target structure was sidestepped by rewording rather than fixed, similar to the avoidance pattern documented in the parent Russian-L1 diagnostic methodology.

responses.given: most students in my class like k-pop

substituted 'hate' for 'doesn't like' — sidesteps the do-support target rather than fixing it (avoidance)

responses.given: my phone is better than yours

responses.note: dropped auxiliary 'is' — partial fix of present continuous

responses.given: i have never drive a car

There are three children in the garden
if you come we will watch movie

responses.given: I hate k-pop i like russian music more. At weekend on saturdays I had English and on sundays I had a Badminton. After my club normally I go outside to play with my friends or do my homework. But if I am not in my country I will call my frieds and we will play online.
